title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
summary: NVIDIA's approach to accelerating video diffusion inference combines quantization, latent caching, and step distillation to reduce denoising steps from fifty to as few as one or four. The techniques are composable, with distribution-based distillation training a student model to match teacher outputs rather than exact trajectories. FastGen packages the post-training and GPU sharding infrastructure to apply these optimizations at scale.
writeup: Ziv Ilan of NVIDIA walks through methods for accelerating video diffusion inference, cutting denoising steps from fifty to as few as one or four. Three composable techniques drive the speedup: quantization, latent caching, and step distillation, where distribution-based distillation trains a student model to match teacher outputs rather than reproduce exact trajectories. The talk also covers FastGen, which packages the post-training and GPU sharding infrastructure to apply these optimizations at scale.
